exemplar.title: To what extent does autism spectrum disorder (ASD) affect emotional intimacy and romantic relationships in adults?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does autism spectrum disorder (ASD) affect emotional intimacy and romantic relationships in adults?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently linked to each section of the essay.
Key constructs and terminology are defined up front, aiding accurate and effective communication throughout.
The methodology articulates clear inclusion criteria and organizes studies into thematic groups, reflecting informed source selection.
The conclusion revisits and synthesizes directly against the stated research question, maintaining a strong sense of focus and purpose.
weaknesses: The phrasing âto what extentâ remains largely qualitative and would benefit from explicit operationalisation or metrics.
Details of database search strategies and exclusion criteria are omitted, reducing transparency and reproducibility.
The scope, while generally maintained, occasionally blends multiple constructs (e.g., satisfaction, intimacy, opportunities) without a single evaluative framework.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation communicates its topic and purpose accurately and maintains a coherent focus, with a clearly stated research question and an organized methodology. Minor breadth issues and lack of explicit search strategy detail prevent a flawless performance, placing the work solidly in the upper end of the 3â6 band but just below the top mark.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent subject knowledge, integrating core psychological concepts (e.g., ASD diagnostic criteria, intimacy, relationship satisfaction) with contemporary literature.
Consistently accurate and precise use of subject-specific terminology and methodological vocabulary.
Source application is coherent and sustained, with each study contextualized clearly in relation to the investigationâs themes.
Critical evaluation of reliability, validity and generalisability concepts deepens the application of sources beyond mere description.
weaknesses: While robust overall, the discussion sometimes slips into descriptive summary rather than deeper theoretical synthesis.
The essay does not incorporate broader psychological frameworks (e.g., attachment, social cognitive theory) that could further enrich conceptual understanding.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits an excellent command of subject-specific knowledge and terminology, with consistently relevant source application and coherent understanding. Minor lapses in theoretical depth are outweighed by the overall strength and precision of knowledge, justifying the top band mark.

strengths: The literature base spans quantitative, qualitative and mixed-methods studies that are directly relevant to the research question, demonstrating good breadth and research quality.
Analysis sections are organized thematically (negative, positive, mixed effects), facilitating clear synthesis of findings.
The conclusion revisits divergent study outcomes, identifies methodological constraints, and proposes concrete future research directions and interventions.
Critical awareness of sample sizes, diagnostic tools and cultural contexts shows an understanding of research rigour.
weaknesses: Analysis often remains at a descriptive level without probing underlying mechanisms or conducting cross-study comparisons of evidence strength.
Discussion of limitations sometimes lists issues without fully evaluating their impact on overall conclusions.
Opportunities to deepen the critique (e.g., exploring confounding variables, comparing cultural moderators) are noted but not fully developed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good research relevance, clear analysis and a reasoned, structured argument with partially critical evaluation. Limitations in analytical depth and uneven evaluation prevent a top-band award, placing the performance firmly within the 7â9 range.

strengths: Follows IB conventions with a clear title page, table of contents, section headings, works cited and appendices.
In-text citations, page numbering, word count and candidate information are all correctly presented.
Figures and tables (e.g., visualisation of qualitative themes) enhance readability and comprehension.
Layout and formatting are neat, with appropriate use of headings and consistent styling.
weaknesses: The Works Cited list shows minor inconsistencies in DOI/URL formatting.
Occasional long URLs break across lines, slightly affecting the visual polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required elements are present and correctly formatted, and the essayâs structure and layout meet the highest expectations. Minor inconsistencies in citation formatting prevent absolute perfection but do not impede comprehension.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Terminology.**  The student employs psychological and clinical terms accurately and consistently: "neurotypical," "cortical thickness," "ASD symptomatology," "standardised questionnaires," etc.  Definitions of constructs (e.g., ASD, emotional intimacy) are supplied, demonstrating command of subject-specific language.

**Conceptual use.**  Concepts such as reliability, validity, generalisability, and triangulation are applied appropriately in study critiques, showing comfort with methodological vocabulary.  Occasional minor lapses (e.g., conflating correlation and causation without full clarification) prevent perfection, but overall usage is **precise and sustained**, fitting the 5-6 band at a score of 5.

**Formal requirements.**  The essay supplies a word count (3 800 words), title, candidate number, and a detailed table of contents.  Page numbers are visible, section headings are consistently formatted, and in-text citations conform broadly to MLA style, with a comprehensive Works Cited list.

**Layout accuracy.**  Minor inconsistencies exist (e.g., occasional long URLs breaking lines, some table formatting issues), yet these do not impede comprehension.  Overall layout is neat, with appropriate use of headings, figures, and tables that enhance readability, fulfilling the descriptors for the top 3-4 band.

**Demonstrated knowledge.**  The essay shows a solid grasp of core psychological conceptsâASD diagnostic criteria, social-communication deficits, intimacy, relationship satisfactionâand integrates them with findings from contemporary literature.  Citations are generally accurate and the student contextualises each studyâs method and outcomes.  

**Application of sources.**  Research is used to construct thematic arguments (negative, positive, mixed effects) and evaluated for validity and generalisability.  Yet discussion sometimes slips into description, and theoretical frameworks (e.g., social cognitive theory, attachment) that could deepen understanding are absent.  The breadth of sources is good but depth of synthesis is moderate.  Hence performance aligns with the **3-4 band, awarded 4**.

**Range and relevance of sources/methods.**  Eight empirical studiesâspanning quantitative, qualitative and mixed methodsâare purposively selected; inclusion criteria and key instruments are tabulated in Appendix A, indicating **informed selection**.  

**Completeness and critical awareness.**  The methodology section acknowledges reliance on secondary research and outlines selection criteria (adult sample, at least one autistic partner, mix of relationship types).  However, there is **no explanation of search strategy, databases, or exclusion criteria**, and only eight studies limits representativeness for an EE of this scope.  The student identifies limitations (e.g., sample sizes, cultural bias) but does not demonstrate how these shaped inclusion decisions.  Overall the methodology is **mostly complete and appropriate but not fully comprehensive**, meriting the higher end of the 3-4 band.

**Clarity and purpose.**  From the opening page the student states the focus on âAutism and Romantic Relationships,â immediately followed by a concise, disciplinary-appropriate research question.  The introduction explains the historical background, defines key constructs (e.g., ASD, emotional intimacy, romantic relationship), and justifies the social relevance of the topic, showing **accurate and effective communication**.  

**Appropriateness of focus.**  The scopeâadult romantic relationships and emotional intimacyâis maintained throughout the essay, as signposted in the table of contents and section headings that mirror the stated purpose (negative, positive, mixed implications â conclusion).  Minor breadth issues remain (e.g., combining several constructs â intimacy, satisfaction, opportunities â into one question) and the phrasing âto what extentâ is not operationalised explicitly, so the focus is clear but not perfectly sharp.  Hence a strong but not flawless performance within the 5-6 band.

**Breadth and relevance.**  All eight core studies directly tackle adult ASD and romantic intimacy; supplementary background sources reinforce key definitions.  The selection covers quantitative and qualitative work across several cultures and years, demonstrating **good breadth and largely consistent relevance** to the research question.

**Quality considerations.**  The student comments on sample sizes, diagnostic criteria, and methodological tools, indicating awareness of research rigour.  Nonetheless the literature base is relatively small and leans heavily on high-functioning ASD samples, so comprehensiveness and cutting-edge coverage are moderate rather than exemplary.  Thus the strand meets the descriptors for the lower end of the 7-9 band.

**Statement and linkage.**  The research question is clearly stated, consistently repeated, and its key terms are defined.  The student links each analytical section back to the question by grouping studies under ânegative,â âpositive,â and âmixedâ effects, showing a **reasonable but not always precise focus**.  

**Limitations of focus.**  The wording âto what extentâ implies a need for measurable comparison, yet the literature review blends prevalence, satisfaction, opportunities, and intimacy without a single evaluative framework; this dilutes precision.  Additionally, adults are treated as a broad category without attention to age ranges or cultural variation promised in the introduction.  Consequently the question is **partially, not fully, focused and connected**, justifying a solid mid-level (3-4 band) award at the upper end.

**Organisational clarity.**  The essay follows IB EE conventions: title page, abstract-style introduction, methodology, analysis sections with sub-headings, conclusion, works cited, and appendices.  The Table of Contents matches page ordering, facilitating navigation.  

**Cohesion and flow.**  Within sections, paragraphs generally progress logically, though some repetition occurs (e.g., restating selection criteria).  Citations and appendices are integrated coherently.  All required elements are present and appropriately ordered, satisfying the expectations of the **3-4 band** at the upper level.

**Discussion structure.**  The conclusion revisits the research question, distinguishes between divergent findings, and acknowledges methodological constraints (e.g., Western bias, high-functioning samples).  The student proposes future research directions (mixed methods, longitudinal designs), evidencing some evaluative thinking.

**Critical evaluation.**  However, evaluation is uneven: some limitations are listed without analysing their impact on conclusions, and alternative interpretations (e.g., cultural moderators, gender identity variables) are noted but not explored.  The argument is logical yet not fully nuanced or deeply critical.  This aligns with the **4-6 band**, and a score of 5 reflects its solid but not sophisticated quality.

**Analytical approach.**  For each study the student summarises findings and offers brief methodological critiques (e.g., sample bias, reliance on self-report).  The synthesis sections (negative, positive, mixed implications) attempt to integrate results and lead logically to the conclusion that effects of ASD on intimacy are variable.

**Depth and support.**  While claims are generally supported by cited evidence, analysis often remains at the level of juxtaposing study outcomes rather than probing underlying mechanisms or theoretical explanations.  Quantitative results are reported descriptively without statistical interrogation, and there is limited cross-study comparison or weighing of evidence strength.  Hence the analysis is **clear and sometimes insightful but not consistently deep or rigorous**, placing it mid-band (4-6) with a score of 5.

annotations.comment: The analysis would benefit from exploring how qualitative methods could enrich understanding beyond self-report questionnaire data.
Highlighting the large sample size in Hancock et al. appropriately supports your evaluation of the studyâs generalisability.
Figure 1 effectively visualises the qualitative process, enhancing presentation and comprehension of complex themes.
The title of the investigation is correctly placed at the start of the report, meeting the formatting requirement.
The word count is provided at the beginning, satisfying the formatting guideline for Extended Essays.
The historical overview of autism demonstrates comprehensive subject knowledge and effective application of multiple sources.
The research question is clearly and prominently stated, providing precise focus and anchoring the investigation in discipline-appropriate terminology.
The definition of romantic relationships is precise and anchored in relevant literature, showcasing accurate use of subject-specific concepts.
The discussion of cortical thickness would benefit from an explicit link to emotional intimacy to reinforce its relevance to the research question.
The causal inference critique could be deepened by identifying potential confounding variables that might explain satisfaction differences.
Critique of Deguchiâs cultural context could be expanded by comparing Japanese societal norms with other settings to strengthen relevance.
The term âto what extentâ in your conclusion remains qualitative; adding specific metrics or comparative measures would sharpen focus.
The prevalence statistic is introduced without evaluating the credibility or methodology of the source, limiting critical depth.
The methodology omits details of database search strategies and exclusion criteria, reducing transparency and reproducibility.
The thematic transition to âPositive Implicationsâ demonstrates coherent structuring and understanding of source applications.
The Works Cited list exhibits inconsistencies in DOI and URL formatting; standardizing these entries would improve presentation quality.
The methodology articulates clear inclusion criteria and organizes studies into thematic groups, reflecting informed selection of sources.
The summary of Bolling (2015) is accurate and demonstrates clear understanding of quantitative instruments and participant selection.
Your critique of Yew et al. notes sample bias but should discuss how this limits transferability of findings to broader ASD populations.
You accurately recognize the methodological balance of quantitative and qualitative data in Strunz et al., enhancing source evaluation.
The focus on high-functioning samples is noted but should be linked back to how this may skew insights relative to your research question.
The conclusion proposes concrete future research directions and interventions, demonstrating evaluative and forward-looking thinking.
The discussion in the Sedgewick et al. section meaningfully highlights gender-specific experiences, adding depth to your analysis.
While you note the limited sample, suggestions for expanding demographic diversity would strengthen your evaluative discussion.
The conclusion directly revisits the research question and synthesizes study findings, maintaining clear focus and purpose.
